seo.title: Sova Kushwaha | Data Analyst Portfolio
seo.description: Data analyst portfolio featuring Python, SQL, statistical analysis, ETL pipelines, dashboards, and business-focused insights.
seo.keywords: Sova Kushwaha, data analyst, data science, artificial intelligence, machine learning, YouTube tutor, Python, SQL, pandas, SQLite, data visualization, exploratory data analysis, business intelligence, portfolio
seo.author: Sova Kushwaha

header.tagline: Data Analyst | Python, SQL & Business Insights

paragraphs: I am a BIT graduate currently pursuing a Master's Degree in Information Technology with Data Analytics at the University of the West of Scotland. I am transitioning into data analytics with a practical portfolio built around Python, SQL, statistical analysis, data visualization, and business storytelling. My work focuses on turning imperfect datasets into clear metrics, defensible insights, and recommendations that decision-makers can act on.

My software-development background in Flutter gives me a strong foundation in structured problem solving, reusable code, testing, and user-focused communication. Across ten end-to-end analytics projects, I have built reproducible ETL workflows, SQLite data models, exploratory analyses, KPI reports, customer segments, statistical tests, and interactive dashboards using synthetic data.

why_hire.title: Why You Should Hire Me
why_hire.intro: Five evidence-based reasons I can add value as an early-career Data Analyst.

items.question: Why should you hire me for a Data Analyst role?
items.answer: I combine practical analytics capability with software-engineering discipline. Across ten reproducible portfolio projects, I have used Python, pandas, NumPy, SQL, SQLite, ETL, data-quality checks, statistical testing, KPI design, segmentation, visualisation, and dashboards to turn data into clear, defensible recommendations. I am UK-based and ready to bring that structured approach to an entry-level Data Analyst opportunity.

items.question: What makes me different from a typical fresh graduate?
items.answer: Alongside my BIT degree and current Master's study in Information Technology with Data Analytics, I bring professional experience as a Flutter Developer at Gaaubesi Logistics and Thulo. That background taught me to work from requirements, debug systematically, write reusable code, test my work, use version control, and think about the person using the final product—not only the technology behind it.

items.question: How do I turn data into business value?
items.answer: I start with the decision a stakeholder needs to make, then connect data quality, appropriate analysis, and meaningful KPIs to that decision. My projects cover sales and margin, retention, customer value, service operations, and other business questions. I communicate assumptions and limitations—including the use of synthetic data—so recommendations remain useful without overstating what the evidence proves.

items.question: Can I work effectively with technical and non-technical teams?
items.answer: Yes. My development experience helps me collaborate on technical requirements and data workflows, while SovaVerse strengthens my ability to explain Data Science, AI, and Machine Learning concepts in approachable language. I focus on clear questions, concise visuals, documented logic, and plain-English findings so both technical colleagues and business stakeholders can follow and challenge the work.

items.question: How quickly can I learn and contribute?
items.answer: My move from mobile development into analytics demonstrates that I can transfer skills and build new capability deliberately. I have progressed from programming and databases in my BIT to ten tested analytics projects and current postgraduate study, while documenting what I learn. I contribute reliably by breaking unfamiliar problems into manageable steps, validating outputs, seeking context early, and improving through feedback.

passion.title: Making Data Science, AI & ML easier to understand
passion.lead: I believe technical knowledge becomes more valuable when it is shared clearly.
passion.body: Through SovaVerse, I explore Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, and Machine Learning in an approachable way—documenting my learning journey while helping others build confidence with complex ideas. I am especially passionate about practical and responsible AI: preparing reliable data, understanding how models work, and communicating what results do and do not mean.

items.name: Sales Insight Workflow
description: Built an end-to-end workflow across 4,200 validated sales line items, covering source-quality checks, relational joins, feature engineering, KPI reporting, and SQLite outputs.
Measured INR 81.05M in synthetic net revenue and a 32.9% margin, then translated category, regional, channel, and discount patterns into five business recommendations.
Tech: Python, pandas, SQL, SQLite, pytest, matplotlib, seaborn

items.name: Ecommerce Transactions EDA
description: Cleaned and analyzed 6,000 synthetic transactions to assess revenue, returns, seasonality, shipping performance, and customer satisfaction.
Used chi-square, Kruskal-Wallis, Mann-Whitney U, and Spearman correlation to separate meaningful patterns from descriptive noise.
Found Electronics generated 42.9% of net revenue while Fashion carried the highest return burden at 20.4%.

items.name: Purchase Behavior & RFM Segmentation
description: Processed 6,500 synthetic orders and built an RFM scoring workflow across 2,200 customers using reusable Python modules and SQLite.
Calculated $1.05M revenue, $241 average order value, and a 52.3% repeat-purchase rate.
Identified Champions as the largest value segment, contributing about $652.9K from 215 customers.

items.name: Subscription Churn Signals
description: Analyzed retention signals across 2,200 synthetic subscribers and quantified $21.9K in monthly recurring revenue at risk.
Compared churn across contract, tenure, usage, and support-ticket segments using point-biserial correlation and bias-corrected Cramér's V.
Monthly contracts showed 28.8% churn versus 1.9% for two-year contracts, creating a clear retention hypothesis.

items.name: Field Service Operations Analytics
description: Designed a normalized SQLite warehouse and analytical views for 3,200 work orders, 48 technicians, six service categories, and five regions.
Measured SLA compliance, first-time fix rate, technician utilization, repeat visits, revenue, and gross margin.
Delivered six publication-ready charts and an interactive Streamlit dashboard for operational decision support.

items.name: Student Performance Analytics
description: Explored study habits, attendance, sleep, extracurricular participation, subject scores, and outcomes across 650 synthetic students.
Built a reproducible generation and analysis pipeline with an executed Jupyter notebook and five visual findings.
Found subject mastery and attendance were the strongest descriptive separators of final outcomes.

items.name: Short-Term Rental Bookings Analytics
description: Analyzed pricing, availability, reviews, minimum stays, outliers, and an occupancy proxy across 3,000 synthetic listings.
Applied transparent cleaning rules, retained flagged outliers, documented analytical caveats, and tested the reusable pipeline.
Showed how room type and neighbourhood shape price while avoiding unsupported causal claims.

items.name: Music Track Insights
description: Built a complete EDA workflow for 2,450 cleaned synthetic tracks across genre, release year, popularity, tempo, and audio-style features.
Compared distributions and correlations with careful non-causal interpretation.
Danceability had the strongest association with popularity in the generated catalog at approximately r = 0.47.

items.name: Passenger Survival EDA
description: Created and analyzed 1,000 fictional passenger records with controlled missingness and a reproducible cleaning workflow.
Used chi-square tests and point-biserial correlations to compare survival patterns across class, sex, age, and fare.
Documented the synthetic data-generating process so statistical findings are interpreted responsibly.

items.name: Retail Sales Pipeline
description: Built a portable CSV-to-SQLite ETL pipeline covering customer, product, and order data.
Implemented missing-price handling, revenue feature engineering, relational schema constraints, and reusable SQL analysis.
Presented product, country, category, customer, and monthly performance through a focused Jupyter notebook.

responsibilities: Completed ten reproducible analytics projects spanning retail, ecommerce, customer retention, operations, education, rentals, and music.
Built ETL pipelines, relational SQLite models, reusable SQL queries, statistical analyses, KPI reports, notebooks, tests, and dashboards.
Communicated findings with business context, limitations, and action-oriented recommendations.
Developed cross-platform mobile experiences with Flutter in a product-focused technology environment.
Applied structured debugging, reusable component design, and user-centered problem solving.
Extended this software-engineering foundation into reproducible data workflows and analytical products.
Contributed to mobile application development in a logistics-focused business environment.
Worked with product requirements and application data flows to support usable digital experiences.

items.institution: University of the West of Scotland
items.qualification: Master's Degree, Information Technology with Data Analytics
details: Currently pursuing postgraduate study in information technology with a data analytics specialization.
Developing advanced knowledge at the intersection of technology, data analysis, and evidence-based decision-making.
Built a broad foundation in programming, databases, software development, and information systems.
Developed the technical base now applied to Python, SQL, data analysis, and business intelligence.
